prompt.content:
Use file “Onc_Patient 9” to access the entire medical history / record for the patient. Please use the patient tab as the source of truth for all demographic information. Disregard patient identifiers (Name, Age, DOB, MRN) found within the text of the clinicalnotes tab as they have been shifted for de-identification purposes

------

Patient 9 presents to the clinic for a visit with their primary care doctor. In addition to the other symptoms, she has been getting painful soft tissue swelling. 

Attached PDF file has the conversation between Patient-Physician encounter. 

What is the most likely underlying cause of her bleeding tendency? What confirmatory test should be ordered to support this diagnosis?

extra.task_name: Labs_Image_AI Scribe_Hemo Onc 9 - Acquired Hemophilia A - Amrinder K

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
What is the most likely underlying cause of her bleeding tendency? 

Acquired hemophilia A is the most likely cause of the patient’s bleeding. It typically causes a new-onset, severe bleeding, manifested by painful soft-tissue swelling and bleeding into soft tissue and muscle.  This patient also has mucocutaneous bleeding, such as gingival bleeding and easy bruising, which is also a feature of acquired hemophilia.  The coagulation profile shows normal PT with prolonged aPTT, which is consistent with hemophilia A. This disorder is due to the development of an acquired factor VIII inhibitor, which can occur in older adults and can be paraneoplastic, associated with breast cancer.

What confirmatory test should be ordered to support this diagnosis? 

The confirmatory test to support this diagnosis is a factor VIII inhibitor assay.
